title: Giant Cell Arteritis
chief_complaint: I have a severe headache on the right side of my head that started 3 days ago. My scalp is tender to touch. When I eat, my jaw gets tired and I can't chew. Yesterday I had blurry vision in my right eye for a few minutes. I've felt generally unwell with muscle aches for weeks.

physical_exam.Temporal_artery: Right temporal artery: Prominent, tender, nodular. Pulsation absent. Left temporal artery: Normal pulsation.
physical_exam.Scalp: Tender to palpation over right temporal region.
physical_exam.Jaw: Claudication reproduced with chewing gum for 2 minutes.
physical_exam.Eyes: Fundoscopy: Right optic disc pale, swollen (anterior ischemic optic neuropathy - AION). Left eye normal. Visual acuity: Right 20/80, Left 20/20.
physical_exam.Musculoskeletal: Tender shoulders and hips (polymyalgia rheumatica symptoms).

hidden_data.cbc: Hb 10.0 (anemia of chronic disease), WBC 11,500, Platelets 450,000 (reactive thrombocytosis)
hidden_data.temporal_artery_biopsy: Transmural inflammation with lymphocytic infiltration. Multinucleated giant cells in internal elastic lamina. Fragmentation of elastic lamina. Luminal narrowing.
hidden_data.fundoscopy: Right eye: Pallid optic disc edema (anterior ischemic optic neuropathy). Cotton wool spots.

correct_diagnosis: Giant Cell Arteritis (Temporal Arteritis) with Anterior Ischemic Optic Neuropathy (Vision-Threatening)
key_learning_points: GCA = granulomatous vasculitis of large and medium arteries, especially cranial branches of aorta. Almost exclusively >50 years, peak 70-80. Female > Male

Clinical: Headache (new onset, temporal), scalp tenderness, jaw claudication (pathognomonic - highly specific), visual symptoms (diplopia, amaurosis fugax, sudden vision loss from AION - IRREVERSIBLE if untreated)
Associated: Polymyalgia rheumatica (50%): Proximal muscle pain and stiffness (shoulders, hips). Constitutional symptoms
Lab: ESR markedly elevated (usually >100), CRP elevated. Normocytic anemia. Thrombocytosis. Diagnosis: Temporal artery biopsy (gold standard) - but DO NOT DELAY TREATMENT for biopsy

Treatment: HIGH-DOSE CORTICOSTEROIDS IMMEDIATELY if clinical suspicion - do NOT wait for biopsy. Prednisone 1mg/kg/day (60-80mg) or Methylprednisolone 1g IV ×3 days if vision threatened. Biopsy within 1-2 weeks (steroids don't affect histology for 14 days). Aspirin 81mg. Tocilizumab (IL-6 inhibitor) for steroid-sparing. Taper steroids over 6-12 months guided by symptoms and ESR/CRP
